sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports intermittent left knee pain, describing a sensation of 'giving out' when kneeling and using a cane to stabilize the knee. He reports pain in the left hand. The patient denies pain. The patient reports knee pain. The patient reports pain lasting only a short time.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient's blood pressure is mildly low. Heart rate is 78 bpm and regular. Neurological exam is nonfocal. The patient has a knee.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has knee pain. The primary problem is knee pain.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The doctor suggests a cane in case. The doctor orders a hemoglobin A1C test. The doctor orders a lipid panel. The doctor orders an echocardiogram. The patient is taking metformin. The patient is taking blood pressure medicine. The doctor recommends physical therapy.
